verdict.answer_first: B opens with the working answer immediately: "giving the client a signed, self-contained token after login"; A first gives structure before the practical flow.
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more decisive with a clear rule: "Single app, one backend? ... probably what you want" and "Distributed systems... JWTs earn their keep."
verdict.fabrication: A is more exposed: "short-lived access tokens (5–15 min)" gives a precise range without evidence; B uses the less brittle "minutes."
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives a concrete production pattern and checklist: "short-lived JWT access token + long-lived, revocable... refresh token" plus validation items.
